---
title: "Quest 11: Recursion - Functions Calling Themselves"
subtitle: "Understand Base Case and Recursive Step"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🔁 QUEST 11 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 10 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [View Notebook](../files/lessons/11-recursion.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/11-recursion.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Recursion means solving a problem by reducing it into smaller copies of itself.
Keep your base case simple and visible.

## 💡 Factorial Example

```rust
fn factorial(n: u64) -> u64 {
    if n <= 1 {
        1
    } else {
        n * factorial(n - 1)
    }
}

fn main() {
    println!("5! = {}", factorial(5));
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Write a recursive function to sum numbers from `1` to `n`.
Test with a small value first, then try a larger one.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn sum_to(n: u64) -> u64 {
    if n == 0 {
        0
    } else {
        n + sum_to(n - 1)
    }
}

fn main() {
    println!("sum_to(6) = {}", sum_to(6));
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 11 Complete
You practiced recursive thinking with a clear base case.
Next: [Quest 12: Complexity](12-complexity.qmd)
:::
